FHA loans across Broward County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, how FHA works from the beaches to the suburbs, and how to get pre-approved.

Broward County Home Loans

FHA loans in Broward County, Florida

Broward County spans glittering beach cities and sprawling family suburbs, from Fort Lauderdale to Pembroke Pines. It's a designated high-cost county, so FHA buyers here enjoy more borrowing room than in most of Florida — useful across a market that ranges from beachfront condos to master-planned single-family communities. Whether you're buying a condo on the coast or a family home out west, FHA's 3.5% down makes Broward more accessible.

Broward County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Broward County

Broward County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000 — above the $541,287 statewide floor — giving you more borrowing room than most of Florida. Because published figures vary for high-cost counties, we confirm Broward County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup before you shop. That higher limit helps across Broward, though luxury beachfront properties can still exceed it.

Beaches, suburbs, and FHA across Broward

Broward gives FHA buyers two clear paths. Along the coast (Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pompano, Deerfield), the market is condo-heavy — confirm the building is FHA-approved or qualifies for Single-Unit Approval via our condo guide. Inland (Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Coral Springs, Plantation, Sunrise, Davie), single-family homes and townhomes are more common and often simpler FHA purchases — ideal for first-time buyers and families.

Cities We Serve

FHA loans in Broward County cities

We help FHA buyers throughout Broward County, including Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Coral Springs, Pompano Beach, Davie, Deerfield Beach, Plantation, Sunrise. Explore our local guides for a closer look at the market, loan limits, and down payment help in each:

Fort LauderdaleHollywoodPembroke PinesMiramarCoral SpringsPompano BeachDavieDeerfield BeachPlantationSunrise

Down payment help in Broward County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Broward County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Broward County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Broward County for 2026?+
Broward is a designated high-cost county, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Can I buy a condo on the Broward coast with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval. Older beach buildings often aren't, so we check first.
Which Broward areas are best for FHA single-family homes?+
Inland cities like Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Coral Springs, Plantation, and Sunrise have more single-family inventory that works well with FHA.
How much down payment do I need in Broward County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score; gift funds and seller credits up to 6% can help.
Do you serve all of Broward County?+
Yes — Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Coral Springs, Pompano Beach, and every community in the county.
